select_when: Performance is expected to improve through repetition, feedback, and chunked skill-building rather than one-off inspiration.
Leaders need to distinguish temporary beginner inefficiency from a capability that compounds with deliberate practice.
The real question is not whether the team can do the work once, but how quickly it can become reliably good at it.
Skill acquisition depends on integrating many specific sub-skills rather than vague general ability claims.
avoid_when: Early progress is being mistaken for mastery and responsibility is scaling faster than capability has actually consolidated.
A familiar framework is being reapplied mechanically because it worked before, even though the current problem may require a new pattern.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on capability ramp quality, anti-false-mastery, and compounding skill acquisition rather than on generic expertise rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor productivity-program sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on repetition, feedback, and the consolidation path to reliable performance.
open_questions: The source also reaches into adaptive strategy and communication, but the operational file stays on capability growth and misread progress.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly says the model is strongest when performance compounds through repetition and feedback and when leaders must judge ramp quality over time.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns about mistaking early progress for mastery and about reusing handy frameworks through substitution bias.

input_type.note: The source is strongest on ramping capability and skill consolidation; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields a compounding path toward reliable performance; the output label compresses that into a short intervention type.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses growth quality and links practices to later capability, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
